revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Itsukahara Kofun]][[ja:五塚原古墳]][[zh:五塚原古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Itsukahara Kofun | image = File:Itsukahara Kofun funkyu-1.JPG | part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qid=Q123117022|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[Muko City]], Terado Town, Shibayama | coordinates = {{Coord|34.949919|N|135.695217|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-26 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] | area = Length 91.2 m
Height 8.7 m (rear circular section) | period = Mid-to-late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|qid=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} | burial = [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Vertical stone chamber]] | buried_person = | relics = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site|1=Historic site}}: "Itsukahara Kofun"
(included in "Otokuni Kofun Cluster") | noteworthy = | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=56|coordinates=59.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=41|lon_sec=42.78|map_dot_label= Itsukahara Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped Kofun-South.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Kyoto Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|56|59.71|N|135|41|42.78|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Itsukahara Kofun''' (五塚原古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in [[Muko City]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Terado Town, Shibayama. It is a [[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] and part of the Muko Hills Kofun Cluster. It has been Historic site a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site|1=Historic site}} of Japan (included in the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster"). This is one of the oldest large keyhole-shaped kofun from the mid-to-late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|qid=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}}, representing the earliest period of the [[Kofun period]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Kofun of the Muko Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief's Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Muko Hills"]}} (Lecture material for the 242nd Kyoto City Archaeological Heritage Lecture, 2013). !No!!name!!type!!area |- |1||'''Itsukahara Kofun'''||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Length 91.2 m |- |2||{{ill|Motoinari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoinari Kofun|qq=}}||[[Square Kofun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]]||Length 94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qid=Q11457885|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Length 95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenzan Kofun (Muko City)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenzan Kofun (Muko City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579270|1=Q135579270}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Length dd4 m |- |5||{{ill|Den-Takabatake Tomb|lt=Denkotabateryo Kofun|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatake Tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||[[Round Kofun|Circular tumulus]]||Diameter 65 m |}
Itsukahara Kofun is located on a ridge in the southern part of the Muko Hills, at the southwestern edge of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. Several other large keyhole-shaped kofun are also distributed in the Muko Hills, collectively referred to as the "Muko Hills Kofun Cluster."{{Sfn|2014 Survey Materials}} Although part of the northwest side of the circular rear section has been leveled for residential development, the overall mound remains well-preserved.{{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Report No. 64|1997|pp=35-37}} Several excavations have been conducted to date. The mound features a southward-facing front section with a "paddle" type typical of early keyhole-shaped kofun.[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20151216000139 "Rear section types a perfect circle; Could this be the prototype of a king's tomb?" Kyoto Shimbun, December 16, 2015.] The mound is built in three tiers in the rear circular section and two tiers in the front section, primarily from packed soil.{{Sfn|2013 Survey Materials}} The surface of the mound contains remnants of stone lining, but no haniwa (clay figures) have been found.{{Sfn|2016 Survey Materials}} The burial chamber is a vertical stone chamber located at the top of the circular rear section.[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20190906000188 "Stone chamber made only of natural stones uncovered for the first time in an early kofun in Muko, Kyoto," Kyoto Shimbun, September 7, 2019.] Additionally, a haniwa coffin has been discovered as a peripheral burial near the west slope of the circular rear section.[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20161013000130 "Was it a chief's fourth-generation relative? Haniwa coffin found at Itsukahara Kofun," Kyoto Shimbun, October 13, 2016.] The construction period is estimated to be the mid-to-late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|qid=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}}, making it the oldest in the Muko Hills cluster of chief's tombs. The "slope-shaped flat surface" of the mound is an old design shared with the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|en|Hashihaka Kofun|qid=Q11603420|lt=Hashihaka Kofun|1=Hashihaka Kofun}} in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}} (believed by some to be Queen [[Himiko]]'s tomb). The site was Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site|1=Historic site}} of Japan in {{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}}.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016. == Excavation History == * {{ill|1967|en|1967|qid=Q2648|lt=1967|1=1967}}-{{ill|1968|en|1968|qid=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}: Survey conducted by Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education.{{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Report No. 64|1997|pp=35-37}} * 1968: Electric survey conducted by Tezukayama University.{{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Report No. 64|1997|pp=35-37}} * {{ill|1977|en|1977|qid=Q2481|lt=1977|1=1977}}: Survey conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory.{{Sfn|2013 Survey Materials}} * {{ill|2000|en|2000|qid=Q1985|lt=2000|1=2000}}-{{ill|2001|en|2001|qid=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}}: First and second excavations conducted by Ritsumeikan University.{{Sfn|2013 Survey Materials}} * {{ill|2013|en|2013|qid=Q1998|lt=2013|1=2013}}: Third and fourth excavations conducted by Muko City designation1 Center and Ritsumeikan University.{{Sfn|2013 Survey Materials}} * {{ill|2014|en|2014|qid=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}}: Fifth and sixth excavations conducted by Ritsumeikan University.{{Sfn|2014 Survey Materials}} * {{ill|2015|en|2015|qid=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}}: Seventh excavation conducted by Muko City designation1 Center.{{Sfn|2015 Survey Materials}} * {{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}}: Designated as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site|1=Historic site}} of Japan. Eighth excavation conducted.{{Sfn|2016 Survey Materials}} * {{ill|2017|en|2017|qid=Q25290|lt=2017|1=2017}}: Ninth excavation conducted by Muko City designation1 Center.{{Sfn|2017 Survey Materials}} * {{ill|2018|en|2018|qid=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}}: Tenth excavation conducted.[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20190906000188 "Stone chamber made only of natural stones uncovered for the first time in an early kofun in Muko, Kyoto," Kyoto Shimbun, September 7, 2019.] == Mound area == The reconstructed area of the mound are as follows:{{Sfn|2016 Survey Materials}} * Mound length: 91.2 m * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 55 m ** Height: 8.7 m ** burial: Three tiers * Front section: ** Length: 40.5 m ** Width: 33 m ** Height: 4.0 m ** burial: Two tiers * Neck section: ** Width: 15 m ** Height: 2.1 m The design is noted for similarities with the {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|qid=Q11528075|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} in Sakurai, Nara.{{Sfn|2015 Survey Materials}} == burial == The burial facility is a vertical stone chamber at the top of the rear circular section.[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/sightseeing/article/20190906000188 "Stone chamber made only of natural stones uncovered for the first time in an early kofun in Muko, Kyoto," Kyoto Shimbun, September 7, 2019.] == Relics == The only confirmed relic is the haniwa coffin found near the west slope of the rear circular section.{{Sfn|2016 Survey Materials}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Itsukahara Kofun: Designated as part of the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" in {{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}}.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 31, March 1, 2016. == Related Links == * {{Commonscat|Itsukahara Kofun}} * {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|en|Hashihaka Kofun|qid=Q11603420|lt=Hashihaka Kofun|1=Hashihaka Kofun}} * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|qid=Q11528075|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|五塚原古墳|version=104109402|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:五塚原古墳]] [[zh:%E4%BA%94%E5%A1%9A%E5%8E%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{Commons category|Itsukahara Kofun}} [[zh:五塚原古墳]] {{Commons category|Itsukahara Kofun}} [[zh:五塚原古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q22117904}} ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
